---
title: 'Master PWD Analysis: Phenotypic Plasticity in CRC'
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-03"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(data.table)
  library(dplyr)
  library(tidyr)
  library(readr)
  library(stringr)
  library(GenomicRanges)
  library(IRanges)
  library(BiocParallel)
  library(utils)
})

# --- Configuration ---
# Parallel workers (adjust based on your RAM)
N_CORES <- 4 
data.table::setDTthreads(N_CORES)

# Standard Chromosomes
STD_CHR <- c(paste0("chr", 1:22), "chrX", "chrY")

# Create data directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists("data")) dir.create("data")

# --- Helper: Download UCSC Data if missing ---
ucsc_db <- "[http://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg38/database](http://hgdownload.soe.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/hg38/database)"
maybe_get <- function(fname) {
  gz <- paste0(fname, ".gz")
  if (!file.exists(fname) && !file.exists(gz)) {
    message("Downloading ", gz)
    download.file(paste0(ucsc_db, "/", gz), destfile = gz, mode = "wb", quiet = TRUE)
    R.utils::gunzip(gz, overwrite = TRUE, remove = FALSE)
  } else if (file.exists(gz) && !file.exists(fname)) {
    R.utils::gunzip(gz, overwrite = TRUE, remove = FALSE)
  }
}
```


## Build Refseq_all
```{r}
# 1. Ensure files exist
maybe_get("knownToRefSeq.txt")
maybe_get("refGene.txt")
maybe_get("ncbiRefSeq.txt")
# Note: "ucsc_hg38_gene_known_genecodeV48" is assumed to be present locally as per your upload.
f_known <- "data/ucsc_hg38_gene_known_genecodeV48" 

# 2. Helper to read RefSeq-like tables
read_ref_like <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) return(data.table())
  
  # Read ALL columns first to avoid 'select' vs 'col.names' conflict
  dt <- fread(path, header = FALSE, sep = "\t", quote = "", na.strings = c("", "NA"),
              col.names = c("bin","name","chrom","strand","txStart","txEnd","cdsStart","cdsEnd",
                            "exonCount","exonStarts","exonEnds","score","name2","cdsStartStat","cdsEndStat","exonFrames"))
  
  # Then select and clean using dplyr (or data.table)
  dt %>%
    as_tibble() %>%
    dplyr::select(name, chrom, strand, txStart, txEnd, cdsStart, cdsEnd, name2) %>%
    mutate(name = sub("\\.\\d+$", "", name))
}

# 3. Read and Merge
message("Building refseq_all...")

# A) Read RefSeq tables
refGene     <- read_ref_like("refGene.txt")
ncbiRefSeq  <- read_ref_like("ncbiRefSeq.txt")

refseq_combined <- bind_rows(refGene, ncbiRefSeq) %>%
  filter(chrom %in% STD_CHR, grepl("^(NM|NR|XM|XR)_", name)) %>%
  distinct(name, .keep_all = TRUE)

# B) Read KnownGene (Gencode/ENST) if available to supplement
if (file.exists(f_known)) {
  known_dt <- fread(f_known, sep="\t", header=TRUE) 
  
  # Normalize columns
  nm <- names(known_dt)
  if(!"name" %in% nm) setnames(known_dt, nm[grepl("name", nm, ignore.case=TRUE)][1], "name")
  known_dt[, name := sub("\\.\\d+$", "", name)]
  
  # Map ENST to RefSeq via knownToRefSeq
  k2rs <- fread("knownToRefSeq.txt", header=FALSE, col.names=c("kgID","refseq_id"))
  k2rs[, `:=`(kgID = sub("\\.\\d+$","", kgID), refseq_id = sub("\\.\\d+$","", refseq_id))]
  
  # Only keep ENSTs that map to a RefSeq ID we don't already have? 
  # For simplicity based on your request, we often prioritize the direct RefSeq tables, 
  # but here we simply output the combined RefSeq table as the master 'refseq_all'.
  # If you specifically need ENST coordinates mapped to RefSeq IDs, logic can be added here.
}

# Final Cleanup of refseq_all
refseq_all <- refseq_combined %>%
  filter(!is.na(name2) & name2 != "") %>%
  mutate(
    txStart  = as.integer(txStart),
    txEnd    = as.integer(txEnd),
    cdsStart = as.integer(cdsStart),
    cdsEnd   = as.integer(cdsEnd)
  ) %>%
  as.data.table()

# Save for future use
# saveRDS(refseq_all, "data/refseq_all.rds")
message("refseq_all built with ", nrow(refseq_all), " transcripts and ", length(unique(refseq_all$name2)), " genes")
```

## Define Pwd intervals
```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# 3. Define Intervals (5'-Most Rule & Mapping)
# ==============================================================================

# --- Helper: Select 5'-most transcript per gene ---
get_5prime_most <- function(dt) {
  # Use strand[1] to handle rare multi-strand annotation artifacts safely
  dt_best <- dt[, {
    if (strand[1] == "+") {
      .SD[which.min(txStart)]
    } else {
      .SD[which.max(txEnd)]
    }
  }, by = name2] 
  return(dt_best)
}

# 1. Filter refseq_all to the single best transcript per gene
refseq_best <- get_5prime_most(refseq_all)
message("Reduced refseq_all to ", nrow(refseq_best), " unique 5'-most genes.")

# --- Define Direct Gene Intervals ---

# A) TSS +/- 500 (1-based coords)
make_tss_window <- function(df, flank=500) {
  df[, {
    tss <- if (strand == "+") txStart + 1L else txEnd
    list(chr=chrom, start=max(1L, tss - flank), end=tss + flank)
  }, by = .(gene = name2)]
}

iv_tss500  <- make_tss_window(refseq_best, 500)
iv_tss1000 <- make_tss_window(refseq_best, 1000)

# B) Transcript Body (txStart to txEnd)
iv_tx <- refseq_best[, .(chr=chrom, start=txStart+1L, end=txEnd, gene=name2)]

# C) CDS (coding sequence only)
iv_cds <- refseq_best[!is.na(cdsStart) & !is.na(cdsEnd) & cdsEnd > cdsStart, 
                      .(chr=chrom, start=cdsStart+1L, end=cdsEnd, gene=name2)]

# --- Helper: Gene Mapping Reference (GRanges) ---
gr_gene_tss <- GRanges(
  seqnames = iv_tss500$chr,
  ranges   = IRanges(start = (iv_tss500$start + iv_tss500$end)/2, width=1),
  gene     = iv_tss500$gene
)

# --- Define Genomic Feature Intervals (cCRE, CGI, TF) ---

# Generic Mapper: Takes any DT with (chr, start, end) and maps to nearest Gene TSS
map_to_nearest_gene <- function(dt, gr_ref) {
  if (nrow(dt) == 0) return(dt)
  
  # Ensure standard chromosomes
  dt <- dt[chr %in% STD_CHR]
  
  # Convert to GRanges
  gr_feat <- GRanges(dt$chr, IRanges(dt$start, dt$end))
  
  # Find nearest gene
  hits <- distanceToNearest(gr_feat, gr_ref, ignore.strand=TRUE)
  
  # Assign Gene and Distance
  dt[queryHits(hits), `:=`(
    nearest_gene = mcols(gr_ref)$gene[subjectHits(hits)],
    dist_to_tss  = mcols(hits)$distance
  )]
  
  return(dt[!is.na(nearest_gene)])
}

# 1. cCREs (Read & Map)
message("Processing cCREs...")
path_ccre <- "data/large_intestine.noccl.cCREs.bed"

if (file.exists(path_ccre)) {
  # Standard BED reading
  dt_ccre <- fread(path_ccre, header=FALSE, sep="\t", fill=TRUE)
  
  # Handle columns safely (SCREEN format usually has 10+ cols)
  # V1=chr, V2=start, V3=end. 
  # cCRE class (pELS/dELS) is often in V10 or V12 depending on version.
  # We will try to auto-detect or default to V10.
  keep_cols <- c("V1","V2","V3")
  if ("V12" %in% names(dt_ccre)) keep_cols <- c(keep_cols, "V12") else if ("V10" %in% names(dt_ccre)) keep_cols <- c(keep_cols, "V10")
  
  dt_ccre <- dt_ccre[, ..keep_cols]
  setnames(dt_ccre, 1:3, c("chr","start","end"))
  
  # Rename class column to 'feature_class'
  if (ncol(dt_ccre) >= 4) setnames(dt_ccre, 4, "feature_class") else dt_ccre[, feature_class := "Unknown"]
  
  # Fix 0-based
  dt_ccre[, start := start + 1L]
  
  # Map
  dt_ccre_mapped <- map_to_nearest_gene(dt_ccre, gr_gene_tss)
} else {
  dt_ccre_mapped <- data.table()
}

# 2. CpG Islands (Read & Map)
message("Processing CGIs...")
path_cgi <- "data/ucsc_cpgislandext.tsv"

if (file.exists(path_cgi)) {
  dt_cgi <- fread(path_cgi, header=TRUE) # UCSC usually has header
  # Standardize names
  setnames(dt_cgi, c("chrom","chromStart","chromEnd"), c("chr","start","end"), skip_absent=TRUE)
  
  # Fix 0-based
  dt_cgi[, start := start + 1L]
  
  # Map
  dt_cgi_mapped <- map_to_nearest_gene(dt_cgi, gr_gene_tss)
} else {
  dt_cgi_mapped <- data.table()
}

# 3. TF Peaks (Robust Custom Reader from User)
message("Processing TFs...")
read_tf_peaks_robust <- function(path) {
  # 1. Read with skip=1 to avoid track header
  dt <- fread(path, sep="\t", header=FALSE, skip=1L, fill=TRUE, data.table=TRUE)
  
  # 2. Assign standard BED names up to what is present
  setnames(dt, paste0("V", seq_len(ncol(dt))))
  bed_cols <- c("chr","start","end","anno","score","strand","thickStart","thickEnd","rgb")
  # Only rename columns that actually exist
  use_cols <- intersect(names(dt), paste0("V", 1:length(bed_cols)))
  setnames(dt, use_cols, bed_cols[1:length(use_cols)])
  
  # 3. Filter / Fix Coords
  dt <- dt[chr %in% STD_CHR & !is.na(start) & !is.na(end)]
  dt[, start := start + 1L] # 0-based to 1-based
  
  # 4. Parse TF Name from 'anno' string (Name=TF_NAME;...)
  # We extract "Name=XYZ" using regex
  dt[, tf_name := str_match(anno, "Name=([^;]+)")[,2]]
  
  # If Name is missing (URL encoded stuff), try fallback or raw cleanup
  # For simplicity in this master script, we take the best attempt:
  dt[is.na(tf_name), tf_name := "Unknown_TF"]
  
  # Decode URL characters if needed (simple version)
  dt[, tf_name := gsub("%20|%28|%29", "", tf_name)] 
  
  return(dt[, .(chr, start, end, tf_name)])
}

path_tf <- "data/Oth.Dig.10.AllAg.AllCell.bed"

if (file.exists(path_tf)) {
  dt_tf <- read_tf_peaks_robust(path_tf)
  dt_tf_mapped <- map_to_nearest_gene(dt_tf, gr_gene_tss)
} else {
  dt_tf_mapped <- data.table()
}
```


## PWD calculation

```{r}
# ==============================================================================
# 4. PWD Calculation Engine
# ==============================================================================

# --- Configuration for PWD ---
MIN_COV  <- 8L
MIN_CPGS <- 1L

# --- Function: Read Methylation BED ---
read_methyl_bed <- function(path) {
  # Expects: chr, start, end, beta, cov, meth, unmeth
  # Reading only essential columns: chr, start, end, beta, cov
  d <- fread(path, select=1:5, col.names=c("chr","start","end","beta","cov"))
  d <- d[chr %in% STD_CHR & cov >= MIN_COV]
  d[, start := start + 1L] # Convert 0-based BED to 1-based R
  setkey(d, chr, start)
  return(d)
}

# --- Function: Calculate Gene-Level PWD ---
# interval_dt must have: chr, start, end, and the column specified by gene_col
calc_gene_pwd_pair <- function(pathA, pathB, interval_dt, gene_col="gene") {
  
  # 1. Read Data
  A <- read_methyl_bed(pathA)
  B <- read_methyl_bed(pathB)
  
  # 2. Intersect Samples (Keep only common CpGs)
  common <- merge(A, B, by=c("chr", "start"), suffixes=c("_A", "_B"))
  if (nrow(common) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 3. Overlap with Intervals (Iterate by Chromosome to save RAM)
  #    This replaces the "interval_chunk" logic with a "chromosome chunk" logic.
  chrs <- intersect(unique(interval_dt$chr), unique(common$chr))
  res_list <- list()
  
  for (k in chrs) {
    # Subset data for this chromosome
    ints_k <- interval_dt[chr == k]
    cpgs_k <- common[chr == k]
    
    if (nrow(ints_k) == 0 || nrow(cpgs_k) == 0) next
    
    # Fast overlap preparation
    cpgs_k[, end := start] # CpGs are points
    setkey(cpgs_k, chr, start, end)
    setkey(ints_k, chr, start, end)
    
    # Overlap (nomatch=0 drops non-overlapping CpGs)
    ov <- foverlaps(cpgs_k, ints_k, nomatch=0L)
    
    if (nrow(ov) == 0) next
    
    # Calculate PWD per CpG site: |BetaA - BetaB|
    ov[, pwd_site := abs(beta_A - beta_B)]
    
    # Aggregate to Gene Level (Summing PWDs and counts)
    # FIX: Use 'by = c(gene_col)' to group by the string name of the column
    agg <- ov[, .(
      sum_pwd = sum(pwd_site, na.rm=TRUE),
      n_cpg   = .N
    ), by = c(gene_col)] 
    
    res_list[[k]] <- agg
  }
  
  if (length(res_list) == 0) return(NULL)
  
  # 4. Combine Chromosomes and Re-aggregate
  # (Re-aggregation ensures genes spanning chromosomes, if any, are summed correctly)
  final <- rbindlist(res_list)
  
  final <- final[, .(
    mean_pwd = sum(sum_pwd) / sum(n_cpg),
    n_cpg    = sum(n_cpg)
  ), by = c(gene_col)]
  
  # Standardize the gene column name to "gene" for downstream compatibility
  setnames(final, gene_col, "gene")
  
  return(final)
}

# --- Wrapper: Run All Pairs (Renamed to match old naming convention) ---
run_all_pairs_one_interval_dt <- function(intervals_dt, feature_name, pairs, bed_paths, gene_col="gene") {
  
  all_res <- list()
  
  for (p_name in names(pairs)) {
    s1 <- pairs[[p_name]][1]
    s2 <- pairs[[p_name]][2]
    
    message("  Processing ", feature_name, " : ", p_name)
    
    # Calculate PWD for this pair
    res <- calc_gene_pwd_pair(bed_paths[[s1]], bed_paths[[s2]], intervals_dt, gene_col)
    
    if (!is.null(res)) {
      res[, pair := p_name]
      res[, feature := feature_name]
      all_res[[p_name]] <- res
    }
    gc() # Aggressively clean memory after each pair
  }
  
  out <- rbindlist(all_res)
  
  # Save immediately to disk (checkpointing)
  if (nrow(out) > 0) {
    save_path <- paste0("data/pwd_", feature_name, ".rds")
    saveRDS(out, save_path)
  }
  
  return(out)
}
```


## Exercution
```{r}
bed_paths <- c(
  EN = "data/EN.bed", IN = "data/IN.bed", JN = "data/JN.bed",
  PA = "data/PA.bed", PB = "data/PB.bed",
  SA = "data/SA.bed", SB = "data/SB.bed",
  XA = "data/XA.bed", XB = "data/XB.bed",
  DA = "data/DA.bed", DB = "data/DB.bed",
  EA = "data/EA.bed", EB = "data/EB.bed",
  FA = "data/FA.bed", FB = "data/FB.bed",
  HA = "data/HA.bed", HB = "data/HB.bed",
  IA = "data/IA.bed", IB = "data/IB.bed",
  JA = "data/JA.bed", JB = "data/JB.bed",
  KA = "data/KA.bed", KB = "data/KB.bed",
  MA = "data/MA.bed", MB = "data/MB.bed"
)

# --- Define Pairs ---
pairs <- list(
  "Normal EN-IN" = c("EN","IN"), "Normal EN-JN" = c("EN","JN"), "Normal IN-JN" = c("IN","JN"),
  "Adenoma PA-PB"= c("PA","PB"), "Adenoma SA-SB"= c("SA","SB"), "Adenoma XA-XB"= c("XA","XB"),
  "CRC DA-DB"    = c("DA","DB"), "CRC EA-EB"    = c("EA","EB"), "CRC FA-FB"    = c("FA","FB"),
  "CRC HA-HB"    = c("HA","HB"), "CRC IA-IB"    = c("IA","IB"), "CRC JA-JB"    = c("JA","JB"),
  "CRC KA-KB"    = c("KA","KB"), "CRC MA-MB"    = c("MA","MB")
)

# --- Run Calculations ---

# 1. Direct Gene Features (use gene_col="gene")
message("Running PWD for TSS500...")
pwd_tss500  <- run_all_pairs_one_interval_dt(iv_tss500,  "TSS500",  pairs, bed_paths, gene_col="gene")

message("Running PWD for TSS1000...")
pwd_tss1000 <- run_all_pairs_one_interval_dt(iv_tss1000, "TSS1000", pairs, bed_paths, gene_col="gene")

message("Running PWD for TxBody...")
pwd_tx      <- run_all_pairs_one_interval_dt(iv_tx,      "tx",      pairs, bed_paths, gene_col="gene")

message("Running PWD for CDS...")
pwd_cds     <- run_all_pairs_one_interval_dt(iv_cds,     "cds",     pairs, bed_paths, gene_col="gene")

# 2. Genomic Features (Mapped to Nearest Gene -> use gene_col="nearest_gene")

# pELS / dELS (Split from cCREs)
# Note: dt_ccre_mapped was created in the previous block.
if (exists("dt_ccre_mapped") && nrow(dt_ccre_mapped) > 0) {
  # The column holding 'pELS'/'dELS' was named 'feature_class' in the previous step
  iv_pels <- dt_ccre_mapped[feature_class == "pELS"]
  iv_dels <- dt_ccre_mapped[feature_class == "dELS"]
  
  if (nrow(iv_pels) > 0) {
    message("Running PWD for pELS...")
    pwd_pels <- run_all_pairs_one_interval_dt(iv_pels, "pELS", pairs, bed_paths, gene_col="nearest_gene")
  }
  
  if (nrow(iv_dels) > 0) {
    message("Running PWD for dELS...")
    pwd_dels <- run_all_pairs_one_interval_dt(iv_dels, "dELS", pairs, bed_paths, gene_col="nearest_gene")
  }
}

# CpG Islands
if (exists("dt_cgi_mapped") && nrow(dt_cgi_mapped) > 0) {
  message("Running PWD for CpG Islands...")
  pwd_cgi <- run_all_pairs_one_interval_dt(dt_cgi_mapped, "CGI", pairs, bed_paths, gene_col="nearest_gene")
}

# Transcription Factors
if (exists("dt_tf_mapped") && nrow(dt_tf_mapped) > 0) {
  message("Running PWD for TFs...")
  pwd_tf  <- run_all_pairs_one_interval_dt(dt_tf_mapped,  "TF",  pairs, bed_paths, gene_col="nearest_gene")
}
```


## Basic summary

```{r}
# Collect all generated objects
all_pwd_frames <- list(
  pwd_tss500, pwd_tss1000, pwd_tx, pwd_cds, 
  if(exists("pwd_pels")) pwd_pels else NULL,
  if(exists("pwd_dels")) pwd_dels else NULL,
  pwd_cgi, pwd_tf
)

# Bind
master_summary_tbl <- rbindlist(all_pwd_frames, use.names=TRUE, fill=TRUE)

# Summarize
summary_stats <- master_summary_tbl[, .(
  N_Genes = uniqueN(gene),
  Mean_PWD = mean(mean_pwd, na.rm=TRUE),
  Median_PWD = median(mean_pwd, na.rm=TRUE),
  Avg_CpGs_Per_Gene = mean(n_cpg, na.rm=TRUE)
), by = .(feature, pair)]

print(summary_stats)

# ==============================================================================
# Visualization
# ==============================================================================
library(ggplot2)

# --- A) Prepare Factors for Plotting ---

# 1. Order 'pair' by the order defined in the 'pairs' list (Normal -> Adenoma -> CRC)
#    Otherwise ggplot defaults to alphabetical.
pair_levels <- names(pairs)
summary_stats[, pair := factor(pair, levels = pair_levels)]

# 2. Order 'feature' logically (CGI -> Promoter -> Enhancer -> Body)
#    Adjust this list based on exactly what features you ran.
feat_levels_priority <- c("CGI", "TSS500", "TSS1000", "pELS", "dELS", "TF", "cds", "tx")
# Keep only levels that actually exist in the data
actual_levels <- intersect(feat_levels_priority, unique(summary_stats$feature))
summary_stats[, feature := factor(feature, levels = actual_levels)]

# --- B) Generate Plot ---

p_trend <- ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = pair, y = Mean_PWD, colour = feature, group = feature)) +
  # Lines connect the SAME feature across different pairs
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.8, alpha = 0.6) + 
  # Points show the actual value
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.9) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mean PWD (mean |Δβ|)",
    colour = "Genomic Context",
    title = "Methylation Conservation Across Sample Pairs",
    subtitle = "Trend of divergence across Normal, Adenoma, and CRC pairs"
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

print(p_trend)

message("Processing Complete. All gene-level PWD files saved to /data.")
```
